description: Trim source_colors.mp4 to only the green segment (seconds 2-4), producing a 2-second video that is entirely green.
requirements: Output video must be exactly 2 seconds long
Every frame must be solid green (no red or blue frames)
Resolution must remain 1280x720
Output must be a valid H.264 MP4 file
expected_issues: Off-by-one errors in trim points causing red or blue frames to bleed in
Using stream copy (-c copy) with non-keyframe start causing corrupted initial frames
Duration being slightly off due to keyframe alignment

description: Reverse source_countdown.mp4 so it counts UP from 1 to 5 instead of down from 5 to 1. The output should show 1, then 2, then 3, then 4, then 5.
requirements: Output video must be exactly 5 seconds long
At t=0.5s the displayed number must be '1'
At t=1.5s the displayed number must be '2'
At t=2.5s the displayed number must be '3'
At t=3.5s the displayed number must be '4'
At t=4.5s the displayed number must be '5'
Resolution must remain 1280x720
expected_issues: Reversed video plays audio backwards if audio track exists
Frame interpolation artifacts when reversing compressed video
Off-by-one in number display due to drawtext expression rounding
Using moviepy reverse without re-encoding causes seek issues

description: Extract the last 2 seconds of source_colors.mp4 (the blue segment, seconds 3-5) and loop it 3 times to produce a 6-second all-blue video with no visible seams between loops.
requirements: Output video must be exactly 6 seconds long
Every frame must be solid blue
No visible jump, flash, or seam at the 2-second and 4-second loop boundaries
Resolution must remain 1280x720
expected_issues: Visible seam/flash at loop points due to re-encoding artifacts
Incorrect extraction range getting partial green frames
Concat demuxer creating timestamp discontinuities
Duration drift causing total length to be slightly off from 6 seconds

description: Speed up source_countdown.mp4 to 2x speed, producing a 2.5-second video where the countdown numbers (5,4,3,2,1) are still clearly readable but pass twice as fast.
requirements: Output video must be approximately 2.5 seconds long (within 0.1s tolerance)
All five countdown numbers must appear and be readable
Numbers must transition at twice the original speed
Resolution must remain 1280x720
No frame blending or motion blur artifacts that make numbers unreadable
expected_issues: Using setpts filter without updating framerate causes dropped frames
Frame blending making numbers partially transparent or overlapped
Audio track (if present) not being sped up in sync
Output duration being slightly wrong due to rounding in PTS manipulation

description: Add a semi-transparent watermark with the text 'DRAFT' rotated 45 degrees diagonally across source_gradient.mp4. The watermark must be visible but not completely obstruct the underlying gradient content.
requirements: Text 'DRAFT' must be clearly readable in every frame
Text must be rotated approximately 45 degrees
Text must be semi-transparent (alpha between 0.3 and 0.7) so the gradient underneath is still visible
Text must be centered or tiled across the frame
The underlying gradient pattern must still be discernible through the watermark
Resolution must remain 1280x720
Video duration must remain 5 seconds
expected_issues: Text not actually rotated (ffmpeg drawtext rotation syntax is tricky)
Transparency not working (opaque white text covering content)
Text positioned outside visible area or clipped at edges
Using PIL overlay in moviepy but not applying it to every frame
Font not found errors on different systems

description: Apply a fade-in effect over the first 1 second and a fade-out effect over the last 1 second of source_colors.mp4. The video should start from black, gradually become visible, and end by fading back to black.
requirements: The very first frame (t=0.0s) must be completely or nearly black
At t=0.5s the frame must be partially visible (dimmed red, not full brightness)
At t=1.0s the frame must be at full brightness
Middle frames (t=2.0s to t=3.5s) must be unaffected
At t=4.5s the frame must be partially visible (dimmed blue)
The very last frame must be completely or nearly black
Video duration must remain 5 seconds
expected_issues: Fade-in applied but fade-out forgotten or vice versa
Fade duration parameters in wrong units (frames vs seconds)
Fade applied to wrong time range (e.g., fade-out starting too early)
Using alpha fade instead of brightness fade, resulting in transparency not darkness

description: Convert source_gradient.mp4 to true grayscale. The output video must have no color information; the R, G, and B channel values of any pixel should be approximately equal.
requirements: All frames must be grayscale: for any pixel, |R - G|, |R - B|, and |G - B| must each be less than 10
The luminance gradient pattern should still be visible (bright areas stay bright, dark areas stay dark)
Resolution must remain 1280x720
Video duration must remain 5 seconds
Output must be a valid MP4 file (not a raw YUV stream)
expected_issues: Using hue=s=0 which may not fully desaturate in all colorspaces
Converting to gray pixel format but then re-encoding to yuv420p without proper mapping
Moviepy ColorClip vs proper luma conversion producing washed-out results
Color fringing due to chroma subsampling in yuv420p after grayscale conversion

description: Create a picture-in-picture composition: overlay a 320x180 version of source_countdown.mp4 in the bottom-right corner of source_colors.mp4 (with a 10-pixel margin from edges). Both videos should play simultaneously from their respective starts.
requirements: Main video (source_colors.mp4) fills the full 1280x720 frame
PiP overlay is exactly 320x180 pixels
PiP is positioned in the bottom-right corner with a 10-pixel margin from the right and bottom edges
PiP shows source_countdown.mp4 content playing in sync from t=0
At t=1.0s: main video is red, PiP shows countdown number '4'
At t=3.0s: main video is green, PiP shows countdown number '2'
Both videos must play for their full overlapping duration (5 seconds)
Output resolution must be 1280x720
expected_issues: PiP overlay positioned incorrectly (wrong corner or wrong offset calculation)
PiP video not scaled to 320x180 (appears at original resolution or wrong aspect ratio)
PiP video not starting at the same time as main video
PiP overlay extending beyond frame boundaries and being clipped
Moviepy CompositeVideoClip vs ffmpeg overlay filter coordinate system confusion

description: Concatenate source_colors.mp4 and source_countdown.mp4 with a 1-second crossfade transition between them. The output should play the colors video, then crossfade into the countdown video over 1 second, then play the remainder of the countdown.
requirements: Output video must be 9 seconds long (5 + 5 - 1 second overlap)
First 4 seconds show source_colors.mp4 content only
Seconds 4.0 to 5.0 show a crossfade blend of both videos
At t=4.5s the frame should show a visible blend of blue (from colors) and the countdown number
From t=5.0s onward, only source_countdown.mp4 content is visible
Both source videos must use their original content (not re-generated)
Resolution must be 1280x720
expected_issues: Crossfade producing black frames instead of blended frames
Crossfade duration being wrong (too short or too long)
Total duration being 10 seconds (simple concat without overlap) instead of 9
Audio crossfade not matching video crossfade
xfade filter offset parameter being miscalculated

description: Create a split-screen video with source_colors.mp4 on the left half and source_countdown.mp4 on the right half, both playing simultaneously. Each half should show its respective video properly scaled to fill 640x720.
requirements: Output resolution must be 1280x720
Left half (pixels 0-639) shows source_colors.mp4 content scaled to 640x720
Right half (pixels 640-1279) shows source_countdown.mp4 content scaled to 640x720
Both halves play simultaneously from t=0
At t=1.0s: left half is red, right half shows countdown number '4'
At t=3.0s: left half is green, right half shows countdown number '2'
No black bars, letterboxing, or gaps between the two halves
Video duration must be 5 seconds
expected_issues: Aspect ratio distortion when scaling from 16:9 to non-standard dimensions
Black bar between the two halves or black border around one half
One video appearing on top of the other instead of side-by-side
Videos not synchronized (one starting before the other)
Pad filter adding unwanted letterboxing

description: Create a 2x2 grid video with four quadrants, each 640x360: top-left is source_colors.mp4, top-right is source_countdown.mp4, bottom-left is source_gradient.mp4, and bottom-right is source_colors.mp4 played in reverse. All four play simultaneously.
requirements: Output resolution must be 1280x720
Top-left quadrant (0,0 to 639,359): source_colors.mp4 scaled to 640x360
Top-right quadrant (640,0 to 1279,359): source_countdown.mp4 scaled to 640x360
Bottom-left quadrant (0,360 to 639,719): source_gradient.mp4 scaled to 640x360
Bottom-right quadrant (640,360 to 1279,719): source_colors.mp4 reversed, scaled to 640x360
All four quadrants must play simultaneously from t=0
At t=1.0s: TL=red, TR=countdown '4', BL=gradient, BR=blue (reversed starts with blue)
At t=3.0s: TL=green, TR=countdown '2', BL=gradient, BR=green (reversed middle)
Video duration must be 5 seconds
expected_issues: Quadrants overlapping or having incorrect positions
Reversed video in wrong quadrant or not actually reversed
Scaling artifacts or aspect ratio distortion in individual quadrants
Complex ffmpeg filtergraph syntax errors with multiple inputs
Memory issues when loading all four video streams simultaneously in moviepy

description: Add a lower-third title bar to source_colors.mp4: a semi-transparent black bar covering the bottom 20% of the frame, with the white text 'Scene 1' centered horizontally and vertically within that bar.
requirements: Output resolution must be 1280x720
A black semi-transparent bar (alpha approximately 0.5) covers y=576 to y=720 (bottom 144 pixels, i.e., 20% of 720)
White text 'Scene 1' is centered horizontally within the bar
Text is centered vertically within the bar (approximately at y=648)
The bar and text are present for the entire 5-second duration
The underlying video content is partially visible through the bar
At t=1.0s: red video visible above bar, red partially visible through semi-transparent bar, white text reads 'Scene 1'
Video duration must remain 5 seconds
expected_issues: Bar is fully opaque instead of semi-transparent
Bar positioned incorrectly (covering top instead of bottom, or wrong height)
Text not centered within the bar (aligned to frame center instead of bar center)
Text too small or too large to be readable
Semi-transparency implemented incorrectly in ffmpeg (requires overlay with alpha)

description: Generate a silent 5-second video (1280x720, black background) with centered white text showing the current timestamp in MM:SS.ms format (e.g., '00:02.340'), updating every frame to reflect the actual playback time.
requirements: Output video must be exactly 5 seconds long at a consistent frame rate (24 or 25 or 30 fps)
Black background, white centered text in every frame
Timestamp format must be MM:SS.mmm (minutes, seconds, milliseconds) or MM:SS.mm
At t=0.0s text shows approximately '00:00.000'
At t=2.5s text shows approximately '00:02.500'
At t=4.9s text shows approximately '00:04.900'
The timestamp must change every frame (not be static or update only once per second)
No audio track, or a silent audio track
Resolution must be 1280x720
expected_issues: Timestamp is static (same value every frame) because the time variable was evaluated once, not per-frame
Timestamp updates only once per second instead of every frame
Incorrect time format (showing frame numbers instead of seconds)
Using moviepy TextClip which renders once and does not update per frame
Millisecond component always showing '000' due to integer division

description: Create a 10-second video that alternates between segments from source_colors.mp4 and black gap frames. Pattern: 2s red, 0.5s black, 2s green, 0.5s black, 2s blue, 0.5s black, then pad remaining time with black to reach exactly 10 seconds. Each color segment should use the corresponding portion of source_colors.mp4.
requirements: Output video must be exactly 10 seconds long
t=0.0s to t=2.0s: red (from source_colors.mp4 seconds 0-2)
t=2.0s to t=2.5s: solid black gap
t=2.5s to t=4.5s: green (from source_colors.mp4 seconds 2-4)
t=4.5s to t=5.0s: solid black gap
t=5.0s to t=6.0s: blue (from source_colors.mp4 seconds 4-5)
t=6.0s to t=10.0s: solid black padding
Resolution must be 1280x720
No audio glitches or pops at segment boundaries
expected_issues: Total duration not exactly 10 seconds due to accumulated rounding errors
Black gaps having wrong duration or being omitted
Color segments taken from wrong time ranges of source video
Timestamp arithmetic errors causing overlapping or missing segments
Concat filter producing timestamp discontinuities visible as frame drops
Blue segment only being 1 second (source only has 1s of blue) but task asks for 2s

description: Take source_countdown.mp4 and add a 1-second freeze-frame at the '3' mark. Specifically, hold the frame at t=2.0s (which shows '3') for an extra 1 second, so the number '3' is displayed for 2 seconds total instead of 1. The resulting video should be 6 seconds long.
requirements: Output video must be exactly 6 seconds long
t=0.0s to t=2.0s: plays normally (shows '5' then '4')
t=2.0s to t=3.0s: shows '3' (first second of '3', from original)
t=3.0s to t=4.0s: freeze frame still showing '3' (the inserted extra second)
t=4.0s to t=6.0s: resumes with '2' then '1'
The freeze must be seamless - no flash, jump, or visual discontinuity at the freeze boundaries
Resolution must remain 1280x720
expected_issues: Freeze frame inserted at wrong timestamp (holding '4' or '2' instead of '3')
Freeze frame creating a visible jump or flash at the splice points
Total duration being wrong (not accounting for the inserted second properly)
Using tpad or loop filters incorrectly causing the freeze to replace content instead of insert
Post-freeze content being shifted by wrong amount, cutting off the final '1'
